Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class MainWindowFactory

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

Factory for creating fully-initialized MainWindow instances with proper dependency injection.

public sealed class MainWindowFactory : IMainWindowFactory
Inheritance
object
MainWindowFactory
Implements
IMainWindowFactory
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a clean abstraction over MainWindow's two-phase initialization pattern, encapsulating the complexity of constructor + Initialize() calls into a single Create() method.

Why This Factory Exists: Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation, preventing direct constructor-based dependency injection. This factory bridges that gap by:

  1. Accepting all dependencies via its own constructor (enabling DI container registration)
  2. Creating MainWindow with parameterless constructor (satisfying Avalonia)
  3. Calling Initialize() with all dependencies (completing setup)
  4. Returning a fully functional window ready for Show()

Dependency Injection Pattern: This factory is registered as a singleton in the DI container and receives all MainWindow dependencies. When Create() is called, it transfers those dependencies to the new MainWindow instance.

Thread Safety: This factory is NOT thread-safe for concurrent Create() calls, but this is acceptable because Create() is only called once during application startup from the UI thread.

Usage Example: 

// In DI container registration (Program.cs or similar):
services.AddSingleton<IMainWindowFactory, MainWindowFactory>();

// In application startup:
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var mainWindow = factory.Create();
mainWindow.Show();

Constructors

MainWindowFactory(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker)

Factory for creating fully-initialized MainWindow instances with proper dependency injection.

public MainWindowFactory(MainWindowViewModel viewModel, VA2M machine, IFrameProvider frameProvider, IRefreshTicker refreshTicker)

Parameters

viewModel MainWindowViewModel

The main window view model containing UI state and commands. Must not be null.

machine VA2M

The Apple IIe emulator instance (VA2M) that executes 6502 instructions and manages system state. Must not be null.

frameProvider IFrameProvider

The frame provider supplying rendered video frames (560x192 pixels) from the emulator to the display control. Must not be null.

refreshTicker IRefreshTicker

The 60 Hz refresh ticker that triggers periodic display updates, ensuring smooth animation and responsive UI. Must not be null.

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a clean abstraction over MainWindow's two-phase initialization pattern, encapsulating the complexity of constructor + Initialize() calls into a single Create() method.

Why This Factory Exists: Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation, preventing direct constructor-based dependency injection. This factory bridges that gap by:

  1. Accepting all dependencies via its own constructor (enabling DI container registration)
  2. Creating MainWindow with parameterless constructor (satisfying Avalonia)
  3. Calling Initialize() with all dependencies (completing setup)
  4. Returning a fully functional window ready for Show()

Dependency Injection Pattern: This factory is registered as a singleton in the DI container and receives all MainWindow dependencies. When Create() is called, it transfers those dependencies to the new MainWindow instance.

Thread Safety: This factory is NOT thread-safe for concurrent Create() calls, but this is acceptable because Create() is only called once during application startup from the UI thread.

Usage Example: 

// In DI container registration (Program.cs or similar):
services.AddSingleton<IMainWindowFactory, MainWindowFactory>();

// In application startup:
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var mainWindow = factory.Create();
mainWindow.Show();

Methods

Create()

Creates a new MainWindow instance and initializes it with all dependencies.

public MainWindow Create()

Returns

MainWindow

A fully initialized MainWindow ready to be shown. All dependencies are injected, reactive subscriptions are set up, position/size restored, and settings loaded.

Remarks

Atomic Operation: This method performs both construction and initialization as a single atomic operation, ensuring the returned window is always in a consistent, fully-initialized state.

Creation Steps:

  1. Call MainWindow parameterless constructor (XAML loading, minimal setup)
  2. Call window.Initialize() with all dependencies (complete setup)
  3. Restore window position/size BEFORE showing (Windows 11 compatibility)
  4. Return fully-initialized window

Windows 11 Position Restore: Window position and size are restored BEFORE the window is shown. This significantly improves the chance that Windows 11 will respect the saved position instead of applying its own "smart" placement algorithm.

Multi-Monitor Support: Validates that saved positions are still on-screen. If the monitor was disconnected or position is invalid, falls back to centered on primary.

What's Initialized:

  • ViewModel and DataContext set
  • Machine and frame provider attached to Apple2Display
  • SoftSwitchStatusPanel initialized with its view model
  • ReactiveUI subscriptions set up (7 property subscriptions + 4 command bridges)
  • Window position/size restored from saved settings
  • Display settings restored from configuration file

Exception Safety: If Initialize() throws an exception (e.g., ScreenDisplay control not found in XAML), the exception propagates to the caller. The partially-constructed window should be discarded.

Usage: 

var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var window = factory.Create();
window.Show(); // Window is ready to show at saved position

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown by MainWindow.Initialize() if called more than once (shouldn't happen in normal usage) or if required controls (e.g., ScreenDisplay) are not found in XAML.
